gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Everything you need to start using MagellAIn on the water.

items.title: Creating Your Account
items.content: Sign up with your email, or use Google or Apple sign-in on the sign-in screen. Once in, go to Menu > My Boats to add your primary boat. MagellAIn uses your boat details to personalize AI coaching, performance analytics, and race data to your specific setup.

items.title: Adding Your Boat
items.content: Go to Menu > My Boats > Add Boat. Enter your boat's name, class (e.g. J/70, S2 7.9, Cal 25), sail number, and PHRF rating if applicable. MagellAIn uses this to tailor performance analytics and AI coaching to your specific boat. You can add multiple boats if you race on different vessels.

items.title: Your Home Dashboard
items.content: The home screen is your at-a-glance command center. It shows current wind conditions from NOAA and NDBC buoys, your next scheduled race, an AI sailing conditions summary, and a live sailing news feed. Tap any card to dive deeper. Sign in to also see your primary boat and personalized data.

items.title: Navigating the App
items.content: MagellAIn uses a bottom navigation bar designed for one-handed use on the water. The five main tabs are: Home (dashboard overview), Map (nautical charts, GPS tracking, and live buoy data), Weather (marine forecasts and wind data), Coach (AI sailing coach with voice input), and Menu (profile, boats, races, safety, settings, and help).

mapAndCharts.title: Map and Charts
mapAndCharts.description: Interactive nautical charts with live data overlays and GPS track recording.

items.title: Using the Map
items.content: The map view centers on your home waters (default: Ford Yacht Club, Lake Erie / Detroit River). Pinch to zoom, drag to pan. The map overlays NOAA electronic nautical charts showing depths, channel markers, buoys, and hazards. All data is specific to Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River.

items.title: Live Buoy Data
items.content: Tap any NDBC buoy marker on the map to see real-time observations: wind speed and direction, wave height and period, water temperature, and air pressure. Key buoys for our area include Station 45005 (W Lake Erie) and Station DTLM4 (Detroit River). Data refreshes automatically every few minutes.

items.title: GPS Track Recording
items.content: The GPS Tracker panel appears in the lower-right corner of the map. Tap 'Start' to begin recording your track — your device GPS will log position, speed, and heading continuously. Live stats show elapsed time, current speed in knots, total distance in nautical miles, and max speed. When finished, tap 'Stop' then 'Save' to store the track. Saved tracks sync to your account when signed in, or save locally if you are offline or not signed in.

items.title: Speedometer and GPS Speed
items.content: The on-screen speedometer displays your current GPS boat speed and heading when location tracking is active. This live data feeds into the AI coach context so you can get condition-specific advice while on the water.

items.title: Race Course View
items.content: When a race course is active, the map displays mark positions, leg distances and bearings, and the current wind overlay. Use this to plan your approach to each mark and visualize wind shifts relative to the course. The tactical analysis panel shows laylines and optimal approach angles.

weather.title: Weather and Wind
weather.description: Detailed marine forecasts interpreted through a sailing lens.

items.title: Weather Dashboard
items.content: The Weather tab is in the bottom navigation bar (third from left). It shows current conditions and forecasts from NOAA/NWS specifically for Lake Erie marine zones (LEZ162 Detroit River, LEZ163 Western Lake Erie open waters). Wind data is displayed in knots with direction, gusts, and a color-coded severity scale matched to Beaufort categories.

items.title: Understanding Wind Data
items.content: Wind is displayed in knots with direction shown as both compass bearing and an animated arrow. The wind trend indicator shows whether conditions are building, steady, or dying. Data is sourced from NDBC buoy observations and NOAA NWS forecast models for the Great Lakes region.

items.title: Weather Alerts
items.content: MagellAIn monitors active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area. Small Craft Advisories, Gale Warnings, and Thunderstorm Warnings appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens. These alerts also feed into the AI coach, which can help you interpret conditions and decide whether to sail.

items.title: Marine Forecast
items.content: View current buoy observations from NDBC stations across Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River, with wave height, water temperature, and barometric pressure. Extended forecasts are sourced from NOAA NWS gridpoint data. Tap 'Refresh' on the weather screen to pull the latest data.

aiCoach.title: AI Sailing Coach
aiCoach.description: Your personal Claude-powered sailing intelligence assistant with voice input.

items.title: Talking to Your Coach
items.content: The Coach tab connects you to MagellAIn's AI sailing coach, powered by Claude. Ask questions in plain English about racing tactics, sail trim, weather interpretation, rules, or boat handling. The coach knows your boat type, your waters, and current conditions, so answers are specific and actionable rather than generic.

items.title: Voice Input
items.content: Tap the microphone button to the left of the text input to speak your question hands-free. A pulsing indicator shows when the coach is listening. Speak naturally — your words appear in the input field as you talk. When you stop speaking, the text is ready to review and send. Voice input requires microphone permission in your browser settings. Available on most modern mobile browsers.

items.title: What You Can Ask
items.content: The AI coach handles a wide range of sailing topics: pre-race strategy ('Wind is 8 knots out of the SW, which end of the line should I start?'), rules questions ('Boat on port tack crossed me at the windward mark, do I have rights?'), trim advice ('My J/70 is slow upwind in 12-15 knots, what should I check?'), weather interpretation ('Is this frontal passage going to shift the wind?'), and post-race analysis ('I finished 4th — what should I work on?').

items.title: Context-Aware Coaching
items.content: The AI coach automatically factors in current weather conditions from your area, your boat's specifications and PHRF rating, and local knowledge about Lake Erie and Detroit River sailing patterns. The more boat and conditions information you share in the conversation, the more specific and useful the advice becomes.

items.title: Chat History
items.content: Your conversations with the AI coach are saved within your session. Start a fresh conversation at any time using the trash icon in the top-right corner of the Coach screen. Sign in to preserve your history across devices and sessions.

racing.title: Race Management
racing.description: Schedules, results, and performance analytics for your racing.

items.title: Race Schedules
items.content: The Races section is accessible from Menu > Races or from the Home screen race card. It shows upcoming and past events from Ford Yacht Club, West Shore Sail Club, and DRYA series. Events include Wednesday Night at the Races (WNATR), weekend regattas, and championship series. Tap any event to expand and see race-by-race results.

items.title: Race Results
items.content: View results from completed races, imported from YachtScoring and club sources. Results show finish positions, corrected times for PHRF handicap races, and series standings by fleet. Tap any race row on the Performance screen to expand the full fleet result for that race.

items.title: Performance Analytics
items.content: The Performance section (accessible from Menu > Performance) shows your race history, season trend, series standings, and head-to-head competitor records for your primary boat. Key metrics include total races, wins, podiums, and average finishing position. GPS track data from the Map screen feeds into speed and distance analytics.

items.title: Series Standings
items.content: Series standings are computed automatically from your race results within each regatta. Points accumulate race by race using corrected position scoring. Your place within the fleet is shown alongside fleet size so you can track your progress through the series. Export a full PDF standings report from the Races screen.

safety.title: Safety Features
safety.description: Emergency tools and safety planning for on-water confidence.

items.title: Emergency Assist
items.content: The Emergency page is accessible from Menu > Emergency. It provides one-tap calling to 911 and direct numbers for USCG Sector Detroit (313-568-9564) and USCG Station Toledo (419-691-4048). Tap 'Get My Location' to display your GPS coordinates in large text for relaying to rescue services. You can also send an SOS text to any saved emergency contact that includes your GPS position.

items.title: Float Plans
items.content: File a float plan before heading out through Menu > Float Plan. Enter your departure location, destination, expected return time, and number of persons aboard. Your vessel details are pre-filled from your boat profile. Shore contact information can be added for someone who should alert authorities if you do not return on time. Active plans are saved and viewable in the Active Plans tab until you close them after returning to dock.

items.title: Weather Safety Alerts
items.content: Active NOAA weather alerts for your sailing area appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens whenever a Small Craft Advisory, Gale Warning, or Thunderstorm Warning is in effect. The AI coach also references active alerts in its responses so you can make informed go/no-go decisions.

items.title: Emergency Contacts
items.content: Go to Menu > Emergency to save personal emergency contacts. Each contact can be called directly or sent an SOS text message that includes your current GPS coordinates. Contacts are synced to your account when signed in. You can also set a primary emergency contact in your profile (Menu > Profile) for quick access.

settings.title: Settings and Preferences
settings.description: Customize MagellAIn to match how you sail.

items.title: Speed Units
items.content: Choose your preferred wind and boat speed units under Menu > Settings > Speed Units. Options include knots (standard nautical), miles per hour, and kilometers per hour. Knots is the default and recommended setting for racing sailors.

items.title: Display Theme
items.content: Switch between Light, Dark, and System themes under Menu > Settings > Appearance. Dark mode is recommended for use on the water as it reduces glare and improves readability in bright sunlight. The System setting automatically matches your device's current display mode.

items.title: Your Profile
items.content: Go to Menu > Profile to update your display name, home club, and emergency contact information. Your profile details help the AI coach calibrate responses to your experience level and local sailing area. Keeping your emergency contact current is important for safety planning.

items.title: Changing Your Password
items.content: If you signed up with email and password, you can change your password under Menu > Settings > Change Password. Enter your new password twice to confirm. If you signed in with Google or Apple, your password is managed by those accounts and cannot be changed here.
